user_stories.goal: Enter destination, trip length, budget level, and interests to generate a personalised day-by-day itinerary
user_stories.benefit: Quickly receive a tailored travel plan that matches my constraints and preferences
acceptance_criteria: There is an input form (website) and a JSON payload (API) that accept destination, trip_length (days), budget_level, and a list of interests
Submitting valid input returns an itinerary containing exactly trip_length days
Each day in the itinerary includes at least one activity, estimated time or duration, and an estimated cost
Returned itinerary respects the selected budget_level and prioritises activities that match the selected interests

user_stories.goal: View the generated itinerary in a clear, day-by-day layout on the website
user_stories.benefit: Easily follow and use the travel plan during the trip
acceptance_criteria: The website displays days in order with activity titles, descriptions, estimated times, and cost per activity
Each activity includes an optional link to a map or external resource when available
The itinerary page is readable on desktop and mobile (responsive)
There is a 'Print / Export' option that produces a printer-friendly view or downloadable PDF

user_stories.goal: Use a Flask API to programmatically generate itineraries
user_stories.benefit: Integrate itinerary generation into other systems or automate workflows
acceptance_criteria: A documented POST endpoint /api/generate accepts JSON with destination, trip_length, budget_level, and interests
The API returns JSON with HTTP 200 and a defined itinerary schema on success and HTTP 4xx with error details for invalid input
The API response includes metadata: generation_timestamp, estimated_total_cost, and confidence_score for recommendations
Rate-limited or well-formed error responses are returned for malformed requests

user_stories.goal: Adjust preferences and regenerate or save different itinerary versions
user_stories.benefit: Iterate on plans without losing prior options and compare alternatives
acceptance_criteria: Users can modify destination, trip_length, budget_level, or interests and request a regenerated itinerary
There is an option to save generated itineraries with a user-visible name and timestamp
Saved itineraries are listed with the ability to restore, delete, or compare two versions
Regenerating does not overwrite saved versions unless the user explicitly chooses to replace them

user_stories.goal: Review and edit generated itineraries before delivering them to clients
user_stories.benefit: Ensure quality and customise proposals to client needs
acceptance_criteria: Admins can view a list of generated itineraries and open any itinerary for editing
Edits allow adding/removing activities, changing times, and adjusting costs with changes tracked
There is an approval or publish action that locks the itinerary for client view and records the approver and timestamp
A basic version history is available showing at least the last three changes with author and timestamp
